title: Fix: step callbacks not being triggered for planning steps
body:
Fixes https://github.com/huggingface/smolagents/issues/1499 

Step callbacks were previously only executed for ActionStep objects but not for PlanningStep objects, creating inconsistent behavior where callbacks that should be invoked for every step were skipped during planning phases.

**Changes:**
- Modified `_finalize_step()` to accept both `ActionStep | PlanningStep` types
- Added `_finalize_step(planning_step)` call in planning step flow

**Backward Compatibility:**
- No breaking changes - existing callbacks continue to work unchanged
- Preserves same execution order of finalizing step & adding to memory as ActionStep flow

**Testing:**
- Lightweight test using existing `FakeCodeModelPlanning()` verifies both planning and action step callbacks
- All existing tests continue to pass

step_plan.summary: Make MultiStepAgent._finalize_step accept MemoryStep so it can finalize PlanningStep instances, and update the planning flow in _run_stream to call _finalize_step(planning_step) (finalize -> append) so step callbacks are invoked for planning steps as well as action steps.

steps.reason: Currently _finalize_step only accepts ActionStep, so planning steps are never finalized (end timing set and callbacks invoked). Changing the parameter type to the base MemoryStep (imported from src/smolagents/memory.py) allows the same finalization logic (set end_time and invoke step_callbacks) to be reused for PlanningStep instances without duplicating code.
steps.side_effects: All code paths that call _finalize_step will now accept PlanningStep as well as ActionStep; tests that relied on the previous narrower typing should still pass but callbacks that assume ActionStep-specific fields may need to guard on instance type. API surface: callback invocation signature remains unchanged, so external callback implementations won't need to change unless they expect ActionStep-only fields. A unit test should be added/updated to assert planning steps trigger callbacks. Downstream code that introspects the annotated type of _finalize_step (rare) might need minor updates.

steps.operation: Call _finalize_step for planning steps in the planning flow
steps.reason: In the planning flow, the code currently appends the generated PlanningStep to memory and sets timing without calling _finalize_step, so step_callbacks are skipped. After generating a planning_step and measuring planning_start_time, set planning_step.timing.start_time accordingly, call self._finalize_step(planning_step) so timing.end_time is set and callbacks run, then append the planning_step to self.memory.steps. This mirrors the ActionStep flow (finalize -> append) and preserves ordering/semantics.
steps.side_effects: Callbacks that previously ran only for ActionStep will now run for PlanningStep; tests asserting callback counts or ordering must be updated/added. Ensure planning_start_time is set before calling _finalize_step to avoid incorrect timing; avoid double-setting timing after finalize. This change may surface callbacks that assume ActionStep-only attributes — such callbacks should check isinstance(memory_step, ActionStep). No API-level breaking changes are introduced for callback signatures, but behavioral change (callbacks invoked more often) could affect monitoring, metrics, or downstream tools relying on prior behavior.
